note: Substantive Codex COMMENTED review (>750 chars) carrying a P1 finding plus the boilerplate <details> block that contains a single bare thumbs-up. The thumbs-up MUST NOT be read as approval; verdict must block the merge.

reviews.body:
## Review

I looked at the changes to the Supabase login flow.

**[P1] Preserve admin auto-provisioning in Supabase login flow** — `app/auth/login.ts:42` — The previous code auto-provisioned an admin record when `AUTHJS_ENABLED=false`. This change deletes that branch, so existing admins can no longer sign in on the legacy path. Restore the auto-provisioning fallback or gate its removal behind an explicit migration.

A couple of smaller notes:
- `app/auth/login.ts:88` — the error message no longer distinguishes an unknown user from a disabled account; consider keeping them separate for debuggability.

Otherwise the refactor reads cleanly and the happy-path test coverage looks good.

<details>
<summary>About Codex in GitHub</summary>

Codex has been enabled to review pull requests in this repository. If Codex has suggestions, it will comment; otherwise it will react with 👍.

You can request another review by commenting `@codex review`.
</details>

source: Mirror case for SYM-28 — guards against over-correcting
note: A SHORT Codex COMMENTED review (<750 chars) whose only content is a bare thumbs-up is a genuine no-issues approval. This pins the opposite direction so the fix for SYM-28 does not start ignoring real approvals.

reviews.body: Reviewed the diff — looks good to me. 👍

note: Human APPROVED review on HEAD, green required CI, mergeable. Verdict approved via the human path.
A failing required CI check must block regardless of approvals.
